source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/03_ADD (HAD1717MCW)/▶ FDA/3차 완료 (최종제출)/VOL_010_Appendix I Non-Clinical Test Report/002_EN 62220-1-1 Test report - HAD1717MCW.docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: EN 62220-1-1 test report for the ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector tested in a configuration containing HAD1717MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory/non-clinical evidence for detector physical, operational, functional, exposure, safety, and image quality characteristics including MTF and DQE measurements.
keyPoints: The report states that MTF and DQE measurements were tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015).
The Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector accessory can be selected between HAD1717MCW and HAD1417MCW, with the report tested in a configuration containing HAD1717MCW.
The device is not for fluoroscopic use and is described as a static x-ray detector fixed at 1 fps.
HAD1717MCW reported DQE values are 50.0% at 0.1 lp, 44.6% at 0.5 lp, 42.7% at 1 lp, 33.7% at 2 lp, and 22.9% at 3 lp.

entities.evidence: Test report was provided for Solid-State X-ray Imaging Devices... Among the accessories of the ADD, the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector can be selected between HAD1717MCW and HAD1417MCW.

entities.name: HAD1417MCW

entities.name: EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03
entities.evidence: 12-289 EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03Medical electrical equipment-Characteristics of digital X-ray imaging devices Part 1-1: Determination of the detective quantum efficiency Detectors used in radiographic imaging.

entities.name: IEC 62220-1
entities.evidence: The measurement procedure described in the report IEC 62220-1 was used...

entities.name: 21CFR1020.31 or .32
entities.evidence: Describe how x-ray beam alignment and collimation to the “active” area of the device will be achieved to meet the requirements of 21CFR1020.31 or .32.

concepts.name: Detective Quantum Efficiency (DQE)
concepts.evidence: Provide an estimate of Detective Quantum Efficiency (DQE) at low spatial and temporal frequency.

concepts.name: Modulation Transfer Function (MTF)
concepts.evidence: Provide a plot of the modulation or contrast transfer function of the device.

concepts.name: Noise Power Spectrum (NPS)
concepts.evidence: The NPS is the noise power spectrum measured at same air kerma.

concepts.name: CsI scintillator
concepts.evidence: Scintillator CsI Converting X-ray to Visible light to charge Phot-Diode

concepts.name: Static x-ray detector
concepts.evidence: This device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.

concepts.name: Active detector area
concepts.evidence: The active area of this device is 425.04 mm x 425.6 mm and is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.

concepts.name: Pixel defect classification
concepts.evidence: Pixel Defect (Cluster) -Criteria : Normal within the criteria

evidence.claim: The report is EN 62220-1-1 Test Report No. A-TCF-5-089 Rev.0.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62220-1-1 Test Report... Report No.: A-TCF-5-089 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: The revision history records Rev. 0 dated Jun/5th/2021 as Establishment.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. No. 0 Rev. date Jun/5th/2021 Revision contents Establishment

evidence.claim: The report covers the ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector and was tested in a configuration containing HAD1717MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: This report has been tested in a configuration containing HAD1717MCW.

evidence.claim: HAD1717MCW and HAD1417MCW are selectable detector configurations for ADD, with performance and usage stated to be the same except for size.
evidence.evidenceText: the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector can be selected between HAD1717MCW and HAD1417MCW. Performance and usage are all the same except for size.
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The applicable standard listed is EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 for determination of detective quantum efficiency of detectors used in radiographic imaging.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03... Determination of the detective quantum efficiency Detectors used in radiographic imaging.

evidence.claim: The detector entire dimensions are 460 mm by 460 mm, with panel size 430 mm by 430 mm and glass thickness 0.7 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Entire dimensions include outer short ring 460 (X) mm x 460 (Y) mm... Panel size... 430 mm x 430 mm... Glass thickness 0.7mm

evidence.claim: The active pixel area size is 425.04 mm by 425.6 mm with 3,036 by 3,040 active area pixels.
evidence.evidenceText: Active pixel area size 425.04 mm x 425.6 mm Number of active area pixel 3,036 x 3,040 = 9,229,440

evidence.claim: The device uses a CsI scintillator to convert x-rays to visible light to charge the photodiode.
evidence.evidenceText: Scintillator CsI Converting X-ray to Visible light to charge Phot-Diode

evidence.claim: The device is not for fluoroscopic use.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not for fluoroscopic.
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.

evidence.claim: All image data are transmitted and stored as 14-bit little-endian two-dimensional arrays without a specific header.
evidence.evidenceText: All image data shall be transmitted and stored as 14-bits little-endian (Intel bytes order) two- dimensional arrays without any specific header.

evidence.claim: The measured HAD1717MCW MTF values are 96.4%, 83.2%, 62.1%, 29.8%, and 15.0% at 0.1 lp, 0.5 lp, 1 lp, 2 lp, and 3 lp respectively.
The measured HAD1717MCW DQE values are 50.0%, 44.6%, 42.7%, 33.7%, and 22.9% at 0.1 lp, 0.5 lp, 1 lp, 2 lp, and 3 lp respectively.
The active area is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: The active area of this device is 425.04 mm x 425.6 mm and is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.

evidence.claim: Readout after x-ray exposure requires at least 10 seconds before the detector is ready for the next exposure, while image readout, transfer, and pre-processing require under 5 seconds.
evidence.evidenceText: Readout of the detector after X-ray exposure requires at least 10 seconds... Image readout, transferring and pre-processing requires under 5 seconds.

evidence.claim: The ADD is stated to meet regulatory standards and IEC standards as outlined in safety report 397950 and EMC reports NK-20-E-0336 and NK-20-E-0337.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD meets the regulatory standards as outlined in the ADD safety report: 397950, EMC report: NK-20-E-0336, NK-20-E-0337... The ADD meets the IEC standards as outlined...

reviewFlags.reason: AuthoritySignal is FDA and the source path includes FDA, but the report body primarily cites EN/IEC standards and 21CFR1020.31 or .32 rather than an FDA-issued document.
Document date is taken from the revision history Rev. 0 date Jun/5th/2021; approval date is separately listed as Jun/7th/2021.
Some formulas and figures appear omitted or degraded in the preprocessed text, including DQE and quantum noise equations.
